Japanese: プルルル‥‥‥‥ プルルル‥‥‥‥
English: Brrring... Brrrrring...

Japanese: ピッ
English: *beep*

Japanese: はい‥‥綾里ですけど。
English: Hello? This is Maya.

Japanese: もしもし。私よ。
English: Hey Maya, it's me.

Japanese: あ、チヒロお姉ちゃん！ どうしたの？　めずらしいね。
English: Mia! What's up? You haven't called in a while.

Japanese: ごめんね。最近、忙しくって。 ‥‥どう？　元気？
English: Sorry, I've been so busy. How you been?

Japanese: もう！　かわいい妹を１人で ほったらかして、ひどいなー。
English: Well, LONELY. And it's all YOUR fault.

Japanese: なーんて。
English: Nah, I'm just teasing.

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよ！　もう、 慣れちゃったから‥‥１人ぐらし。
English: I've been great! I'm finally getting used to having my own place.

Japanese: そう‥‥ありがとう。
English: That's good to hear.

Japanese: ‥‥あのね。実はちょっと、 たのみたいことがあるの。
English: Actually, I'm calling because I have a favor to ask.

Japanese: あー、わかった！　また、 証拠品あずかれって言うんでしょ？
English: I know, I know. You want me to hold evidence for you?

Japanese: よくわかったわね。
English: Sharp as always!

Japanese: ‥‥今度の事件、ちょっと 話が大きくなっていてね。
English: There's a lot of buzz about the upcoming trial...

Japanese: 裁判の日まで、そばに 置いておきたくなくて。
English: I just don't feel safe keeping the evidence here.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。 で？　で？　今度は何？
English: I gotcha. So, what is it this time?

Japanese: 置き時計よ。
English: It's... a clock.

Japanese: ‥‥時計？
English: A clock?

Japanese: そう。考える人の形をしてて、 なんと、しゃべるのよ！
English: Yeah, it's made to look like that statue, "The Thinker." And it tells you the time!

Japanese: あなた、好きでしょ？ こういう、オモチャみたいなの。
English: I thought you might like it. You always liked toys.

Japanese: あー。またあたしのコト、 コドモ扱いしたー。
English: Hey! I'm not a little girl anymore, Sis!

Japanese: ふふふ。 ‥‥ごめんなさい。
English: Now, now. You know I'm only teasing.

Japanese: ‥‥あ。でも、今はこの時計、 しゃべらないんだったっけ。
English: Ah, I should probably tell you, the clock isn't talking right now.

Japanese: えー、なんでー？ ‥‥つまんない。
English: Huh? It's not working? That's lame!

Japanese: さっき、 時計の機械を抜いちゃったから。
English: I had to take the clockwork out. Sorry.

Japanese: かわりに、書類が入っているの。
English: I put some papers inside it instead.

Japanese: 書類？ ‥‥それが“証拠品”なの？
English: Papers? Is that the evidence, then?

Japanese: うー。なんか、 話がソレっぽくなってきたね。
English: Hmm, well... there's a possibility that it might turn out that way, yes.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、今晩の９時ごろ、 事務所に来てくれるかしら？
English: Can you come by the office tonight, say 9:00, to pick it up?

Japanese: 裁判の打ち合わせ、 たぶん、そのころまでかかるから。
English: I'll be in a pretrial meeting until then.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん！　また、何か おいしいのゴチソウしてよね！
English: Okay, Sis, but I expect dinner! Something good!

Japanese: あ、みそラーメンがいいな みそラーメン。
English: Like... burgers! I could really go for a good burger.

Japanese: はいはい。 じゃ、いつものお店、行こうね。
English: Okay, okay. We'll hit the usual joint.

Japanese: やったー！　ヤクソクだよ！
English: Alright! It's a deal!

Japanese: じゃ、お姉ちゃん。 また、あとでね！
English: Okay, Sis, see you soon!

Japanese: うん。 待ってるわよ、マヨイ。
English: Yep. I'll be waiting, Maya.

Japanese: ピー
English: *beep*

Japanese: 《ただ今の通話、録音しました。 ９月５日、午前９時２７分》
English: [Conversation recorded. September 5, 9:27 AM]

Japanese: ９月　５日　午後８時５７分 綾里法律事務所
English: September 5, 8:57 PM Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、ミス・チヒロ。 もらっていくよ‥‥書類を。
English: Now, Miss Fey, I'll take what's mine... the papers.

Japanese: 悪いけど、渡せないわ。 ‥‥ここには、ないもの。
English: I'm sorry, but I can't give you what I don't have.

Japanese: ミス・チヒロ‥‥、 ウソはヘタみたいだね。
English: Miss Fey, you are a poor liar.

Japanese: ちゃんとあるじゃないか、 そこに‥‥。
English: Why, I see it right over there...

Japanese: 書類を飲み込んだ 《考える人》が、さ。
English: That must be "The Thinker" that swallowed those papers.

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どうしてそれを！
English: How could you know...?

Japanese: ははっ。 ドンチュノウ？　知らないのかい？
English: Ho hoh. You are not cogniferous of my background?

Japanese: ぼかぁ、情報を集めるのが 仕事なんだよ。
English: Gathering information is my business, you see.

Japanese: 油断‥‥したわ。
English: I... I should have been more careful.

Japanese: ‥‥フン。
English: Ho hoh.

Japanese: それと‥‥ ちょっと気の毒なんだけど、
English: My dear Miss Fey... I am so very sorry.

Japanese: キミには、約束してもらわなきゃ ならないことがあるんだ。
English: But I am afraid I must ask you for one more thing.

Japanese: エターナル・サイレンス‥‥ 永遠の沈黙、をね。
English: Your eternal silence... Farewell, Miss Fey.
Red... White... Blue...

Japanese: ９月　５日　午後９時　８分 綾里法律事務所
English: September 5, 9:08 PM Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: ふう。 すっかり遅くなっちまった。
English: Uh oh, I'm late.

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいな。 所長、帰っちゃったのかな。
English: Huh, that's strange. The chief must have gone home already.

Japanese: 妹さんが遊びにくるから、 ゴハン食べに行こうって‥‥。
English: She said her sister was coming over so we should all go out for dinner...

Japanese: なんだ‥‥このニオイ‥‥。
English: What's that smell...?
Blood...?

Japanese: ち、千尋さん！ （所長室か！）
English: Mia! (Maybe she's in her office!)

Japanese: （さ、さっきの女の子は ‥‥どこだ！）
English: (That girl just now... where'd she go!?)

Japanese: （そこのソファに 寝かせておいたのに‥‥！）
English: (I put her right there on that sofa...!)

Japanese: （まさか‥‥逃げた、のか？）
English: (Uh oh... I hope she didn't run on me.)

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Yipes!

Japanese: （‥‥ふう。　おどかすなよ‥‥）
English: (Don't scare me like that...)

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。
English: Umm...

Japanese: 名前‥‥教えてくれないかな？
English: Excuse me but, who are you?

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。 ぼくは、この事務所の人間だから。
English: It's okay. I work here.

Japanese: マヨイ‥‥。
English: Maya...

Japanese: アヤサト・マヨイ。
English: Maya Fey.

Japanese: あやさと‥‥まよい‥‥。
English: Maya... Fey?

Japanese: （“マヨイ”だって‥‥？）
（千尋さんが書き残したのは、 この子のことだったのか‥‥？）
English: (So Mia was writing this girl's name!)

Japanese: （ちょっとこの子に、あの領収書を つきつけてみよう）
English: (Maybe I should show her the receipt?)

Japanese: （法廷で覚えたテクニックが、 実生活で役に立つとは‥‥）
English: (I never thought there'd be a use for evidence like this outside the courtroom!)

Japanese: ファン‥‥ファン‥‥ ‥‥ファン‥‥ファン‥‥
English: *whee-ooo* *whee-ooo*... *whee-ooo* *whee-ooo*...

Japanese: パ、パトカーだ！ （ここへ向かってるみたいだ‥‥）
English: The police! (Sounds like they're coming this way!)

Japanese: 動くな！　警察だ！
English: Freeze! Police!

Japanese: 自分は、刑事課の イトノコギリという者ッス。
English: Alright, I'm Detective Dick Gumshoe, see?

Japanese: （イトノコギリ‥‥ ヘンな名前だな）
English: (Gumshoe...? What an odd name.)

Japanese: このビルの向かいの ホテルの客から、
English: We received a report from the building across the way, see.

Japanese: 殺人を目撃したという通報が 入ったので、かけつけたッス。
English: Got a person saying they saw a murder.

Japanese: （‥‥そうか。あの女の人だな）
English: (It must have been that woman I saw.)

Japanese: とりあえずアンタたち、 そこを動いちゃダメッスよ。
English: Anyway, I don't want either of you moving one inch, 'kay?

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Great. Just great.)

Japanese: （マヨイ‥‥ちゃんか。 まさか、この子が‥‥？）
English: (Maya... Wait, she wouldn't have... nah.)

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Whoaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ちょっといいッスかッ！
English: Scuze me!

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: この“マヨイ”という文字に 心当たりはないッス？
English: This word "Maya" here mean anything to you?

Japanese: あ。あの‥‥それ‥‥。
English: Um... that...

Japanese: あ‥‥あたしの名前‥‥
English: That's my name...

Japanese: なんスとぉぉぉッ！
English: WHAAAAT!?

Japanese: これは、ヒガイシャが血で 書いたメモッス！
English: The victim drew this here note in her own blood, see?

Japanese: 息を引き取るまぎわ、 ハンニンを告発したッス！
English: With her dying breath, she wrote down the killer's name!

Japanese: あ‥‥あたしじゃありま
English: K-killer...? I'm not--

Japanese: こりゃもう、キマリッス！ 署まで来てもらうッス！
English: Case closed! You're coming down to the precinct, ma'am.
W-what?

Japanese: 千尋さんの妹・真宵ちゃんは、 緊急逮捕された。
English: Mia's younger sister, Maya, was arrested on the spot.

Japanese: ぼくも取り調べを受けて、 解放されたのは、明け方だった。
English: I was taken in for questioning and didn't get out until the next morning.

Japanese: まぶたは重かったが、 眠れるわけがない。
English: My eyes were heavy... but I couldn't sleep.

Japanese: 留置所の面会が始まる 時間を待って、
English: I sat around, waiting for visiting hours to begin at the detention center.

Japanese: ぼくは、すぐに真宵ちゃんに 会いに行った。
English: I had to talk to Maya as soon as possible.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、早く 警察を呼ぼう！）
English: (I'd better hurry up and call the police!)

Japanese: 所長室へ通じるドアだ。 半開きになっている。
English: The door to the chief's office. It's slightly open.

Japanese: ドアノブには 触れない方がいいだろう。
English: I'd better not touch the door knob.

Japanese: 受付に使っているデスクだ。 いつもぼくが座っている。
English: The reception desk. I usually sit here.

Japanese: 来客用のソファだ。 革張りの高級品らしい。
English: This sofa is for clients. It's leather... a real luxury model.

Japanese: 大きな絵画だ。 モダン・アートというのだろうか。
English: A large painting. I guess you'd call this "modern art."

Japanese: ぼくには、ただの 失敗作にしか見えない。
English: I, on the other hand, call it a mistake.

Japanese: 法律関係の本が、イヤになるほど ビッシリ並んでいる。
English: You couldn't cram more legal books in here, even if you wanted to.

Japanese: 事務所を訪れる客たちは、 かならずこの本棚にビビる。
English: Few can gaze upon the shelves without feeling insignificant.

Japanese: 小さなライティングデスクには、 事務用品が整然と並んでいる。
English: A small writing desk cluttered with office supplies.

Japanese: ‥‥今は、この血のニオイが 気になる！
English: I smell blood... and that can't be good!

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥所長の無事を たしかめないと！
English: I have to check and see if Mia... the Chief's okay!

Japanese: 名前、教えてくれないかな。
English: Excuse me but, who are you?

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。 ぼくは、この事務所の人間だから。
English: It's okay. I work here.

Japanese: マヨイ‥‥。
English: Maya...

Japanese: あやさと・まよい。
English: Maya Fey.

Japanese: （“マヨイ”だって‥‥？）
（千尋さんが書き残したのは、 この子のことだったのか‥‥？）
English: (So Mia was writing this girl's name!)

Japanese: （ちょっとこの子に、あの領収書を つきつけてみよう）
English: (Maybe I should show her the receipt...)

Japanese: （法廷で覚えたテクニックが、 実生活で役に立つとは‥‥）
English: (I never thought there'd be a use for evidence like this outside the courtroom!)

Japanese: （かなり弱っているみたいだけど、 聞かないわけにはいかないな‥）
English: (She seems to be in shock. I don't want to disturb her, but I have to know...)

Japanese: あの‥‥きみ。
English: Um... excuse me?

Japanese: いったい、何が あったんだい？
English: Can you tell me what happened?

Japanese: ‥‥あたしが来たら、 部屋が暗くて‥‥
English: I came in... The room was dark.

Japanese: もう、お姉ちゃんは‥‥
English: And Sis... Sis...!

Japanese: （亡くなった後だった、 というわけか）
English: (So she was already dead.)

Japanese: きみは、所長の‥‥？
English: So, you're the chief's...?

Japanese: ‥‥妹です。
English: Sister. I'm her younger sister.

Japanese: こんな時間に‥‥遊びに来たの？
English: And you were here... visiting? This late at night?

Japanese: はい‥‥。 証拠品をあずかって欲しい、って。
English: Yes. She said she wanted me to keep some evidence for her.

Japanese: 証拠品‥‥って？
English: Evidence...?

Japanese: はい‥‥。 あ、あの‥‥、
English: Yes... I-it was that clock...

Japanese: 《考える人》の置時計を‥‥。
English: It was "The Thinker."

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。 特に心当たり、ないです。
English: I'm sorry, I've never seen that before.

Japanese: これ、所長のそばに 散らばってたんだけど‥‥
English: This was lying next to the chief.

Japanese: はい。 あたしも見ました。
English: I know. I saw it there too.

Japanese: たぶん、電気スタンドの カケラかな、って。
English: I thought they might be pieces of the light stand.

Japanese: うん。たぶんね。
English: Hmm. Maybe.

Japanese: （でも、ガラス製の電気スタンド なんて、めずらしいな‥‥）
English: (Never heard of a glass light stand before...)

Japanese: あの、《考える人》の ことなんだけど‥‥
English: Um, about "The Thinker"...

Japanese: い、いや！
English: The Th-Thinker...

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃんは、あれで‥‥！
English: That was... Oh, Sis! *sob*

Japanese: （今、凶器のことを聞くのは さすがにヒドいな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, probably shouldn't have asked her about the murder weapon...)

Japanese: 千尋さん、亡くなる前に 自分の血でメッセージを‥‥
English: Before Mia died, she wrote a message with her own blood.

Japanese: 領収書のウラにね。
English: She wrote it on the back of this receipt.

Japanese: あ、あ、あたしの名前！
English: Th-th-that's MY name!

Japanese: ど、どうして！ お姉ちゃんが‥‥？　どうして！
English: W-why!? Why would she write my name?

Japanese: お、落ち着いてくれ！
English: Please, just calm down.

Japanese: なんで‥‥お姉ちゃんが、 あたしを‥‥？
English: W-why would Sis write my name?

Japanese: （う。そ、そんな目で 見られてもなあ‥‥）
English: (Uh-oh. Now I've done it...)

Japanese: ９月　６日　某時刻 綾里法律事務所
English: September 6 Fey & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: （事務所は警官たちで いっぱいだ‥‥）
English: (The office is filled with police officers.)

Japanese: （みんな、忙しそうに 動きまわっているぞ‥‥）
English: (They're all busily searching for clues...)

Japanese: コラ！
English: Hey! You there!

Japanese: ここは立入禁止ッス！
English: This is a crime scene, pal! No trespassing!

Japanese: ‥‥ん？ たしかアンタは‥‥、
English: Umm... Sorry, don't I know you from somewhere?

Japanese: ヤッパリくん‥‥ だったッス？
English: Wait, you're that Butz guy, aren't you!

Japanese: ナルホドです！ （アイツと間違えないでくれ！）
English: No, no, Phoenix Wright. (How could anyone mistake me for Larry!?)

Japanese: あ、そうそう。 なんかそんな名前だったッス！
English: Ah, guess I got the wrong name, Mr. Wright. Sorry 'bout that.

Japanese: “ヤッパリ”は、たしか 殺人犯の名前だったッス。
English: That Butz guy, he was a killer! And you're no killer! Right?

Japanese: （アイツたしか、 ムザイになったはずだけど‥‥）
English: (He WAS proven innocent...)

Japanese: えーと。 そういうあなたは、たしか‥‥
English: Umm... right. And you were...

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥ でしたよね。
English: Um... Gumshoe, wasn't it? Dick Gumshoe?

Japanese: そッス。 イトノコッス‥‥。
English: Right! At your service.

Japanese: ってアンタ！　人の名前、 勝手に縮めちゃダメッス！
English: Hang on! That's Detective Gumshoe to you, pal!

Japanese: えーと、カメノコ刑事‥‥ でしたっけ。
English: Um... Suedeshoes, wasn't it?

Japanese: そッス。 カメノコッス‥‥。
English: That's me! Don't step on my blue suede shoes...

Japanese: って、それはタワシッス！
English: Wait! That's a song, pal!

Japanese: 自分は、イトノコ‥‥
English: My name's Dick Gumshoe...

Japanese: いやいやいや。
English: W-wait!

Japanese: そもそも、人の名前を 勝手に縮めちゃダメッス！
English: That's Detective Gumshoe to you, pal!

Japanese: えーと、ノコノコ刑事‥‥ でしたっけ？
English: Um... Gumtree, was it? Detective Gumtree?

Japanese: ノ、ノコノコ‥‥。
English: G-Gumtree...?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ、ちょっと おふざけがすぎたッス‥‥。
English: Gum doesn't grow on trees, pal!

Japanese: 本官をナメると、イタい目に あうッスよ‥‥！
English: And I'm the one in charge here so don't push your luck!

Japanese: （うわあ、急に自分のコト “本官”とか言い出したよ‥‥）
English: (Whatever you say, Detective...)

Japanese: 自分は、糸鋸　圭介 （いとのこぎりけいすけ）ッス！
English: Anyway, get the name right.

Japanese: なんぴとたりとも、 自分を“イトノコ”なんて‥‥
English: And don't go calling me "Dick"...

Japanese: おい、イトノコ！ こっち、早くたのむよ！
English: Hey, Dick! Get over here!

Japanese: ハ、ハッ！
English: Y-yes, sir!

Japanese: す、すぐ行くッス！
English: B-be right there!

Japanese: あー、オホン！
English: Umm... ahem!

Japanese: たしかアンタ、 弁護士だったッスな。
English: You're her lawyer, right, pal?

Japanese: 用なら、早くしてほしいッス！
English: If you got business here, you'd better do it quick!

Japanese: （しめた！　ぼくを真宵ちゃんの 弁護士だと思っているぞ‥‥！）
English: (Whew! He thinks I'm Maya's lawyer...!)

Japanese: （‥‥だいたい、聞くべきことは おさえたかな‥‥）
English: (I guess I've asked all the questions I need to.)

Japanese: もういいッス？
English: You all done, pal?

Japanese: あ。ありがとうございました。 ‥‥そろそろ、帰ります。
English: Um, yes, thank you. I'll be heading out now.

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そうそう。
English: Oh, wait.

Japanese: あんたに、１つ言っておくッス。
English: One more thing I wanted to mention to you.

Japanese: おおかた、これからあの 目撃証人に会おうとか、
English: I don't suppose you're planning on talking to that witness.

Japanese: そんなフラチなことを 考えてるかもしれないッスが、
English: Anyway, you'd better not!

Japanese: 会ってもムダッスよ！
English: No influencing the witness with your lawyerly ways, pal!

Japanese: （そういえば、彼女のコト、 忘れてたな‥‥）
English: (Come to think of it, I had completely forgotten about her...)

Japanese: 目撃証人、ですか‥‥？
English: The... witness?

Japanese: 松竹　梅世（しょうちくうめよ） のことッス！
English: Yeah, Miss April May.

Japanese: あいにくッスが、
English: I'm sorry 'bout this...

Japanese: 自分は、彼女の情報は ゼッタイもらさないッス！
English: But I can't tell you anything about her!

Japanese: （名前は松竹　梅世、か）
English: (Well, you just told me her name. Miss May, huh?)

Japanese: もう彼女、家に帰らせて あげたんですよね？
English: So you've sent her home already, then?

Japanese: ワレワレをナメちゃ ダメッス。
English: Ahah! You're trying your lawyerly tricks on me now!

Japanese: 彼女には、裁判が終わるまで、 勝手な外出は禁止してるッス！
English: She's not to go outside her room until the trial!

Japanese: （‥‥てことは、まだ彼女は この向かいのホテルにいる、と）
English: (So... she's still in the hotel across the way.)

Japanese: はあ。 さすが警察ですね。
English: I guess I should know better than to try to get a detective to leak information.

Japanese: わかればいいッス！
English: You got that right, pal!

Japanese: （よし！　ホテルにも 行ってみよう！）
English: (Time to pay a visit to Miss May!)

Japanese: 所長室へ通じるドアだ。
English: The door to the chief's office.

Japanese: ドアの向こうでは、警官たちが 捜査をしているようだ。
English: The police are in there searching for clues.

Japanese: ‥‥入らない方がいいな。
English: I'd better not go in.

Japanese: 千尋さんお気に入りの 観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.

Japanese: 名前は、とても常人には おぼえられそうもないような‥‥
English: I remember it had this bizarre name no one could ever remember.

Japanese: “コルディリネ・ストリクター” ッス！
English: "Cordyline stricta," pal!

Japanese: （誰だ、今の声‥‥）
English: (Who was that!?)

Japanese: 法律関係のムズカシイ本が いやというほど並んでいる。
English: There's a horrendous amount of legal books here.

Japanese: 恐ろしいことに、千尋さん、 この本を全部、読んでいるそうだ。
English: Scarier still is that Mia probably read all of these.

Japanese: ぼくの気持ちとウラハラに、 そとはいい天気だ。
English: The sky is blue, and so am I...

Japanese: 目の前に、例のホテルが見える。
English: There's that hotel, right across the way.

Japanese: 千尋さんのデスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: いつもどおり、 キレイに片づいている。
English: Perfectly clean, as always.

Japanese: 足りないのは、千尋さんの 姿だけ、だ‥‥。
English: The only thing it's missing is... Mia.

Japanese: 古い映画のポスターだ。
English: An old movie poster.

Japanese: 千尋さんが初めて泣いた 映画なのだそうだ。
English: Apparently, this was the first movie that made Mia cry when she saw it.

Japanese: 今度、ぼくも見てみるか。
English: I'll have to check it out one of these days.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥ 解剖は、したんですか‥‥？
English: About Ms. Fey... did you do an autopsy?

Japanese: なんス？ 知りたいッスか？　結果？
English: Hmm? You want to know the results, eh?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そんな目で見つめちゃ ダメッス。
English: Now don't you look at me like that, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥そッスね‥‥。
English: It's no use!

Japanese: あんたは、彼女のもとで 働いていたこともあるし、
English: She might have been your boss, but that doesn't mean you get any special treatment.

Japanese: 報告書を見せてあげるッス。
English: Alright, alright. You can see the report, but that's all!

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥即死だったんですか。
English: So, Mia's death was instantaneous.

Japanese: ‥‥そッスね。
English: That's right, pal.

Japanese: キレイなヒト、だったッス‥‥。
English: She was... a beautiful person.

Japanese: あれ。刑事さん、所長のこと 知ってるんですか？
English: You knew her, Detective?

Japanese: 警察の関係者で、彼女のことを 知らない人間はいないッス。
English: Sure. All of us down at the precinct knew Ms. Fey.

Japanese: くやしいッスけど、彼女は エラい弁護士だったッス。
English: Much as it pains me to say this, she was a darn good lawyer.

Japanese: ‥‥い、いや、何でもないッス。 め、目にホコリが入っただけッス。
English: Hey, what are you looking at? I just got a little dust in my eye, that's all.

Japanese: あの、真宵さんのこと なんですけど‥‥。
English: Um, about Maya...

Japanese: ああ。 裁判が楽しみッスね。
English: Yeah! I'm looking forward to the trial!

Japanese: あんたには気の毒ッス。 ゼッタイに勝てないッス！
English: Sorry, pal, but this is one trial you aren't going to win!

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: W-why do you say that?

Japanese: 検事局は、この事件の担当に、 あの御剣検事を任命したッス！
English: The city's put Prosecutor Edgeworth on the prosecution!

Japanese: （‥‥ミツルギ‥‥）
English: (Edgeworth...)

Japanese: これがどういうことか、 弁護士ならわかるッスね？
English: I'm sure you know what that means, you being a lawyer and all.

Japanese: ミツルギ検事‥‥。
English: Prosecutor Edgeworth...

Japanese: そッス。御剣　怜侍 （みつるぎれいじ）その人ッス！
English: That's right, pal! Mr. Miles Edgeworth himself!

Japanese: まさか、知らないなんてこと、 ないッスよね？
English: Wait... you do know him, don't you!?

Japanese: 有罪判決を獲得するためには 手段を選ばない、
English: I know him. He's a feared prosecutor. He doesn't feel pain, he doesn't feel remorse.

Japanese: 血も涙もない、オニのような 検事‥‥ですよね。
English: He won't stop until he gets his "guilty" verdict.

Japanese: ヒト聞きの悪いこと、 言っちゃダメッス。
English: Aww, don't talk about him that way. You make him barely sound human!

Japanese: ‥‥でも。これで、もう勝負は 決まったようなものッス。
English: Still, I'm afraid this pretty much decides the case.

Japanese: （御剣検事が出てくるのか‥‥）
English: (So, Edgeworth is on this one...)

Japanese: （２０歳という、異例の若さで 検事になって以来、負け知らず）
English: (He hasn't lost a case since he became prosecutor at the incredibly young age of 20.)

Japanese: （黒いウワサも絶えない。 裏取引や証拠のでっち上げ‥‥）
English: (Of course, there are rumors of back-alley deals and forged evidence...)

Japanese: （そして、ほとんど異常ともいえる “犯罪”に対する憎しみ‥‥）
English: (All I know for sure is that Edgeworth hates crime with an almost abnormal passion.)

Japanese: 聞いたこと、ないですけど‥‥。
English: Never heard of him.

Japanese: はん！ あきれた弁護士ッスね。
English: Whoa! And you call yourself a lawyer, pal!?

Japanese: ２０歳で検事になってから４年。
English: About four years ago, this Edgeworth guy became a prosecutor at the age of 20!

Japanese: 検事局始まって以来の天才と 言われている彼を知らないとは！
English: Everyone says he's a genius. Surprised you don't know him!

Japanese: （‥‥知らないワケ、ないだろ。 ちょっと、トボけてみただけさ）
English: (Of course I know him... I was just playing dumb.)

Japanese: （有罪判決を獲得するためには なんでもする、冷酷な男！）
English: (He's a cold, heartless machine who'll do anything to get a "guilty" verdict!)

Japanese: （裏取引や証拠のでっち上げ‥‥ 偽証させるってウワサもある）
English: (There are rumors of back-alley deals and forged evidence...)

Japanese: （ほとんど異常ともいえる、 “犯罪”に対する憎しみ‥‥）
English: (All I know for sure is that Edgeworth hates crime with an almost abnormal passion.)

Japanese: （まさか、こんなに早く 会うことになるとは‥‥）
English: (I never imagined I'd be facing him so soon...)

Japanese: ‥‥なんスか、それ？
English: Hmm? What's that?

Japanese: 悪いッスが、アンタに やる情報なんか、ないッス！
English: Sorry, pal, but I got no info for the likes of you!

Japanese: あの。綾里　真宵の 携帯電話のことなんですけど‥‥。
English: I was wondering... did you see Maya Fey's cell phone?

Japanese: ああ。 自分があずかっているッス。
English: Oh that? I have that.

Japanese: できれば、返して もらいたいんですけど。
English: Do you think you could give it back?

Japanese: ‥‥！ あやしいッス！
English: Sure! I mean, wait a second, pal!
Tricky lawyer!

Japanese: （うわあ‥‥。どうしよう！）
English: (Uh-oh, he's on to me!)

Japanese: （ダメだ！　正直に話したら、 ゼッタイ返してくれないぞ！）
English: (Hmm... if I tell him why I want it there's no way he'll give it to me!)

Japanese: どうしたッス？
English: Something the matter?

Japanese: あ、いや、その‥‥。 わかるでしょう、刑事さん？
English: Oh, no, it's just... You know, Detective!

Japanese: いいや。 わからねッス！
English: Nope. I know nothing, pal!

Japanese: その電話には、あの子の ボーイフレンドの番号とか、
English: That cell phone has a lot of numbers on it... like her boyfriend's...

Japanese: あま酸っぱいオトメのヒミツが いっぱい、つまってるんですよ。
English: A cell phone holds all a little girl's sweetest and spiciest secrets!

Japanese: ハッ。ワレワレ警察を ナメてもらっちゃ困るッス！
English: Urk? Y-you're trying to confuse me!

Japanese: 悪いッスが、メモリーされた番号は すべて、チェックしたッス！
English: Sorry, pal. I already checked all the numbers in memory!

Japanese: さ、さすが警察！ すばやいですね‥‥。
English: Impressive! You're quite the detective.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いいッス。 返すッス。
English: Uh huh. Oh, here, you can have the phone back.

Japanese: あいにく、あやしい電話番号は なかったッスから。
English: There weren't any suspicious call records in there, after all.

Japanese: （うーん‥‥。 ごまかすって言ってもなあ‥‥）
English: (Okay, I can't be straight with this guy... but what should I tell him?)

Japanese: どうしたッス？
English: Something the matter?

Japanese: あ、いや。そ、その。
English: Oh, no, um...

Japanese: そ、その携帯電話についている ストラップなんですけど‥‥。
English: T-that carrying strap on the cell phone...

Japanese: 《大江戸戦士・トノサマン》って プリントされているッスね‥‥。
English: This? Hmm... it says "The Steel Samurai: Warrior of Neo Olde Tokyo"...

Japanese: トノサマン‥‥、たしかテレビの 人気ヒーローだったッスな？
English: "The Steel Samurai"... that action hero on TV?

Japanese: そ、そのストラップ、なかなか 手に入らないものらしくって。
English: Yeah, you see that strap is a collector's item...

Japanese: 警察にあずけておいて、 なくなったらイヤだわ、って。
English: She... was worried it might get lost if it went down to the precinct.

Japanese: あの子が言ってるッスか。
English: That what she said?

Japanese: まあ、そうです。
English: Um. Yes.

Japanese: いいッス。
English: Okay, pal.

Japanese: メモリーされた電話番号は ひかえたッスから、
English: I wrote down all the numbers she called anyway.

Japanese: 返すッス。
English: Here you go.

Japanese: （録音された会話には 気づかなかったみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Seems he didn't notice the recorded conversation...)

Japanese: 《真宵の携帯電話》を 返してもらった。
English: Received: Maya's Cell Phone.

Japanese: 法廷記録で、録音された 会話を聞くことができる。
English: Check the Court Record to hear the recorded conversation.

Japanese: 刑事さん‥‥ あの、これなんですけど‥‥。
English: Detective Gumshoe? Could you take a look at this?

Japanese: なんスか？ 電話ならもう返したッス。
English: Huh? I already gave you the girl's cell phone, pal.

Japanese: （よし。後で録音された会話を 聞いてみよう‥‥）
English: (Right. I'll have to listen to that recorded conversation later.)

Japanese: こいつなんですけど‥‥ 何かわかりましたか？
English: I was wondering, do you know anything about this?

Japanese: それは‥‥、 凶器の置物ッスね。
English: That statue? That's the murder weapon.

Japanese: （あれ？　置時計のことを、 この刑事も“置物”だって）
English: (Huh? He thinks the clock is just a statue, too...)

Japanese: （よくよく不運な時計だよ、 コイツは‥‥）
English: (I'm starting to wish I'd never seen this thing.)

Japanese: 現場には、 ガラスが散らばってましたよね？
English: There was broken glass at the crime scene, right?

Japanese: ああ。あれッスか。
English: Hmm? Oh, that?

Japanese: 死体のそばに、ガラスで できたスタンドが倒れていたッス。
English: Seems like a glass stand next to the victim fell over.

Japanese: あのガラスは、 そのスタンドの破片ッス。
English: The glass shards were pieces of the broken stand.

Japanese: 死体のそばに、紙切れが 落ちてましたよね？
English: There was a piece of paper next to the victim, wasn't there?

Japanese: ああ。犯人の名前が 書いてあった、あれッスか。
English: Yeah. The one with the killer's name written on it!

Japanese: あの文字、間違いなく 千尋さんが‥‥？
English: Are you sure that Mia wrote it herself?

Japanese: 残念ながら、あんな状態では 筆跡の鑑定はできないッス。
English: Given the condition of the writing, it's hard to say if it's her handwriting or not.

Japanese: （千尋さんが書いたという 証拠は、ないわけか‥‥）
English: (So there's no proof that Mia wrote it.)

Japanese: これ、ありがとうございます。
English: Thanks for this!

Japanese: “弁護士とは、正々堂々たたかう” が、ワレワレのテーマッス。
English: Don't mention it, pal.

Japanese: 気にすること、ないッス。
English: When it comes to dealing with lawyers, "fight fair and square," is our motto!

Japanese: （でも、こういう書類に まとまっちゃうと‥‥）
English: (I don't know how I feel, seeing everything written up like this...)

Japanese: （所長の死がひどく事務的な ものに見えて、悲しいな‥‥）
English: (It makes Mia's death seem so... routine.)

Japanese: （どうやら、イトノコ刑事は 帰ってしまったみたいだな）
English: (Looks like Detective Gumshoe has gone home.)

Japanese: （あいかわらず、所長室では 警察官たちが忙しそうだ）
English: (The police are still keeping themselves busy in the chief's office.)

Japanese: （誰も相手をしてくれそうにない。 ‥‥退散したほうがいいかな）
English: (No one has time to talk to me. Guess I'll head out.)

Japanese: 千尋さんお気に入りの 観葉植物だ。
English: Mia's favorite potted plant.

Japanese: これから、ぼくが 水をやることになるのか‥‥？
English: I guess I'll have to water it now...

Japanese: ‥‥血のニオイだ！
English: That smell... Blood!
*sob*

Japanese: ‥‥お姉ちゃあん‥‥
English: Sis...

Japanese: （‥‥だれか、いる！）
English: (Someone's there!)

Japanese: しょ、しょ、‥‥
English: Chief? Chief...?

Japanese: 所　　長　　！
English: Chief!!!

Japanese: きみは‥‥？
English: Who are you?

Japanese: （‥‥気絶した少女を 事務室のソファに寝かせて、）
English: (The strange girl dropped out cold. I left her lying on the office sofa.)

Japanese: （ぼくは所長のそばに戻ってきた）
English: (I went back to the chief where she lay under the window.)

Japanese: （‥‥所長の身体には、 まだ少しぬくもりが残っている）
English: (Her body was still warm...)

Japanese: （肩を抱くぼくの手を通して はっきり感じることができる）
English: (I could feel it when I held her shoulder.)

Japanese: （でも、そのぬくもりは、 急速に、そして確実に、）
English: (Then, all too quickly, it began to fade...)

Japanese: （この手から失われていった‥‥）
English: (Until finally she was cold.)
Chief...

Japanese: 大小のガラスの破片が 散らばっている。
English: Some shards of glass are scattered on the floor.

Japanese: どうやら、ガラスでできた 電気スタンドだったようだ。
English: They seem to be the remains of a glass light stand.

Japanese: この事務所のすぐ目の前に、 ビルが見える。
English: There's a large building right across from the office.

Japanese: “板東ホテル”という ビジネスホテルだ。
English: The "Gatewater Hotel." A nice, luxurious place.

Japanese: 血がベットリついている。 なんともヒニクなことに‥‥
English: It's encrusted with dried blood.

Japanese: 今度は、所長の命を 奪ってしまったらしい。
English: How ironic that this became the murder weapon... again.

Japanese: 手さげ金庫だ。
English: A portable safe.

Japanese: いくら入っているのかは わからない。
English: No way to tell how much is inside.

Japanese: 誰か持ち出そうとしたのだろうか。
English: I wonder if someone was trying to take it out?
Chief...

Japanese: 見るのもツラいけど、 調べないわけにはいかないな。
English: It's hard seeing her like this, but if there are any clues here...

Japanese: ‥‥鈍器で頭部を殴られている。
English: She was struck on the head with a blunt object.

Japanese: たぶん、即死だったんじゃ ないだろうか。
English: She probably died instantly.

Japanese: 死体のそばに転がっている 《考える人》が凶器か。
English: "The Thinker" lying next to her must have been the murder weapon.

Japanese: 証拠品《考える人》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: The Thinker added to the Court Record.

Japanese: それと‥‥所長の身体のまわりに、 ガラスのカケラが散らばっている。
English: Hmm... there are some glass shards near the chief's body.

Japanese: 部屋の奥にたおれている ガラス製の電気スタンドの破片か？
English: Must be pieces of the glass light stand lying broken in the back of the room.

Japanese: 証拠品《ガラスの破片》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Glass Shards added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥他にはもう、手がかりに なるようなものは‥‥
English: Nothing else that seems like a clue here...
Hmm...?

Japanese: 千尋さんの身体から、 何か紙切れが落ちたぞ。
English: A piece of paper! It must have fallen from Mia's hand!

Japanese: なんだろう？
English: What could it be?

Japanese: 見るのもツラいけど、 調べないわけにはいかないよな。
English: It's painful to look... but I have to, for Mia's sake.

Japanese: ‥‥鈍器で後頭部を殴られている。
English: Looks like she was hit in the head with a blunt weapon.

Japanese: たぶん、即死だったんじゃ ないだろうか。
English: She probably died instantly.

Japanese: 小さな紙切れに、血で文字が 書かれている！
English: A word is written in blood on this scrap of paper!

Japanese: “マヨイ”‥‥だな。 千尋さんが書いたのか‥‥？
English: "Maya"...? Did Mia write this?

Japanese: ちなみにこの紙切れは、デパートの 領収書で、昨日発行されたものだ。
English: This piece of paper is a receipt from a department store, dated yesterday.
(Wait a second!)

Japanese: （“マヨイ”だって‥‥？）
（千尋さんが書き残したのは、 あの子のことだったのか‥‥？）
English: (Was Mia trying to tell us something about that girl?)

Japanese: （ちょっとあの子に、この領収書を つきつけてみよう）
English: (I think I'd better show her this receipt.)

Japanese: （法廷で覚えたテクニックが、 実生活で役に立つとは‥‥）
English: (I never thought there'd be a use for evidence like this outside the courtroom!)

Japanese: 証拠品《領収書》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Receipt added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （とりあえず、調べるのは これぐらいでいいだろう）
English: (I think that's enough snooping around for now.)

Japanese: （警察に通報‥‥、それに、）
English: (I'd better call the police...)

Japanese: （あの女の子のことも 気になるな‥‥）
English: (and find out what that girl was doing here.)

Japanese: そうだ！ とにかく、警察に通報しよう。
English: Right! I'd better call the police!

Japanese: この電話、ヘンだな。
English: That's funny...

Japanese: 受話器のネジが、いくつか はずされている。
English: A few of the screws on the receiver are missing...

Japanese: 何かの作業を、とちゅうで やめたような感じだ。
English: It looks like someone was halfway through taking it apart.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥来てぇっ！
English: Police!?

Japanese: 早く来てぇっ！　おまわりさぁん！
English: Please, come quick!

Japanese: （な、なんだなんだ！）
English: (Wh-what was that!?)

Japanese: （窓の向こうで叫び声が！）
English: (Someone screaming from outside the window!?)

Japanese: こ、こっちをじっと 見つめているぞ！
English: She's staring right at me!

Japanese: ‥‥受話器を にぎっているような‥‥。
English: She's holding a phone in her hand...

Japanese: この電話‥‥受話器のネジが いくつか、はずされている。
English: The phone receiver is missing a few screws.

Japanese: ‥‥使わない方がいいな‥‥。
English: I'd better not use it.

Japanese: 綾里法律事務所の帳簿だ。
English: The Fey & Co. ledger book.

Japanese: 所長のキレイな字で 数字がビッシリ書かれている。
English: Everything is written in the chief's ultra-neat handwriting.

Japanese: 小さなオフィスだけど、 けっこう、もうかってるみたいだ。
English: It's a small office, but it makes a good bit of money.

Japanese: ちょっと意外なことに、 所長はキカイに弱い。
English: Surprisingly, the chief was never good with machines.

Japanese: このパソコンも、ほとんど 電子メール専用に使っていた。
English: About all she used this PC for was e-mail.

Japanese: とても古いモデルを、タダで もらってきたそうだ。
English: She picked up this ancient model at some garage sale for practically nothing.

Japanese: ごくふつうのオフィス用 デスクだ。
English: A perfectly normal office desk.

Japanese: 依頼人も使う家具には タップリお金をかけて、
English: The chief had a very particular policy about office décor:

Japanese: 自分が使うものはシンプルに‥‥ というのが、所長の方針だ。
English: "Spend big on stuff the clients use, but keep your own stuff simple."

Japanese: 所長の大切な資料が ぎっしり並んでいる。
English: All the chief's important documents are packed in here.

Japanese: 事件の調書や、最近の判例なんかが ファイルされているそうだ。
English: This is where she filed her case records and recent rulings.

Japanese: 所長のイスだ。
English: The chief's chair.

Japanese: 機能重視の、シンプルな デザインで、座りやすいんだ。
English: A simple, functional design. Feels pretty good to sit in, too.

Japanese: ９月　６日　午前９時　７分 留置所　面会室
English: September 6, 9:07 AM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥ すっかり犯人あつかい、か‥‥）
English: (Wow, they have poor Maya locked up like a criminal.)

Japanese: ‥‥あ。 あのときの‥‥弁護士さん。
English: Oh!
It's you! The lawyer...

Japanese: お、おはようございます。
English: G-good morning.

Japanese: おはよう！
English: Good morning!

Japanese: （かわいそうに‥‥。ずいぶん、 つかれているみたいだ）
English: (She looks so tired...)

Japanese: あの‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: あなたが、あたしの弁護士さんに なるんですか‥‥？
English: Are you going to be my attorney?

Japanese: え。そ、それは‥‥
English: Well, that's what I wanted to talk to you about...

Japanese: （ここは、マジメに 答えるべきだな‥‥）
English: (I'd better give it to her straight...)

Japanese: ‥‥きみしだい、だよ。
English: It's up to you.

Japanese: あたし‥‥ですか？
English: Up... to me?

Japanese: うん。ぼくが決めることじゃ ないと思うよ。
English: Yes. I don't think this is something I should decide.

Japanese: ‥‥これは、きみの問題だからね。
English: After all, you're the one in trouble here.

Japanese: 信じてもらえるわけ、 ないもん‥‥あたしのこと。
English: They're never going to believe me... are they?

Japanese: あのときの、弁護士さんの目。 “こいつが犯人だ”って‥‥。
English: Even you, when you found me in the office. You looked at me like I had done it!

Japanese: （‥‥彼女を見たとき、ぼくは そんな顔をしてたのか‥‥）
English: (Did I look at her like that?)

Japanese: い、いや！　そんなことは‥‥。
English: No, no! I never thought...

Japanese: あ。‥‥いいんです。 ムリしなくても‥‥。
English: I-it's okay. I understand.

Japanese: うん。‥‥それに、 あたし、聞いちゃったから。
English: And... I've also heard about you.

Japanese: え？　何を？
English: Heard...? Heard what about me?

Japanese: 実は、この前、お姉ちゃんと 電話で話したの‥‥。
English: I... was talking to my sister on the phone the other day...

Japanese: （何はともあれ、ここは 元気づけてあげるべきだよな）
English: (First things first, I better get her cheered up...)

Japanese: ああ。もちろんそうだよ！ だから、元気出して！
English: Yeah, of course I will! Cheer up!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうなんですか。
English: R-really...?

Japanese: （な、なぜだ！　さらに 悲しげなカオになっちまった！）
English: (Whoa! Did I say the wrong thing? She looks sadder now!)

Japanese: あれ‥‥ど、どうしたのかな？ ぼくじゃ、不安、とか‥‥？
English: Um... what... what's wrong? You don't think I can do it?

Japanese: ‥‥だって。
English: No... no one could!

Japanese: 信じてもらえるわけ、 ないもん‥‥あたしのこと。
English: Who would believe me?

Japanese: あのときの、弁護士さんの目。 “こいつが犯人だ”って‥‥。
English: Even you, when you found me in the office. You looked at me like I had done it!

Japanese: （‥‥彼女を見たとき、ぼくは そんな顔をしてたのか‥‥）
English: (Did I look at her like that?)

Japanese: い、いや！　そんなことは‥‥。
English: No, no! I never thought...

Japanese: あ。‥‥いいんです。 ムリしなくても‥‥。
English: I-it's okay. I understand.

Japanese: うん。‥‥それに、 あたし、聞いちゃったから。
English: And... I've also heard about you.

Japanese: え？　何を？
English: Heard...? Heard what about me?

Japanese: 実は、この前、お姉ちゃんと 電話で話したの‥‥。
English: I... was talking to my sister on the phone the other day...

Japanese: （よし。ジョークの１つも飛ばして 元気づけてあげるとするか）
English: (Maybe if I joke a bit she'll cheer up...)

Japanese: はっはっは。 ぜーったい、ゴメンだね！
English: Hah hah! No way, Jose!

Japanese: なーんて。
English: Just kidding...

Japanese: （うわわわ、しまった。 さすがに、シュミが悪すぎたか）
English: (Eh heh. Whoops. That didn't go so well.)

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: あ、あの。
English: M-Maya...?

Japanese: 今、心なしか“ホッ”と しなかった？
English: Was that a... chuckle?

Japanese: えっ？
English: What?

Japanese: あ、い、いえそんな‥‥！
English: N-no!

Japanese: ば‥‥バレちゃいました？
English: It wasn't very believable, was it?

Japanese: （な‥‥なんでだあ‥‥）
English: (Not really...)

Japanese: あ、す、すいません！
English: I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: せっかく力づけようとして くださってるのに‥‥
English: I just thought, since you'd made a joke...

Japanese: あの、気にしないで がんばってください！
English: Please, don't mind me! You're doing just fine!

Japanese: （こっちが元気づけられて しまった）
English: (Who's trying to cheer up who here!?)

Japanese: ‥‥だって。
English: I knew it.

Japanese: し‥‥信じてもらえるわけ、 ないもん‥‥あたしのこと。
English: No one will believe me.

Japanese: え？
English: What?

Japanese: あのときの、弁護士さんの目。 “こいつが犯人だ”って‥‥。
English: Even you--when you found me in the office. You looked at me like I had done it!

Japanese: （‥‥彼女を見たとき、ぼくは そんな顔をしてたのか‥‥）
English: (Did I look at her like that?)

Japanese: い、いや！　そんなことは‥‥。
English: No, no! I never thought...

Japanese: あ。‥‥いいんです。 ムリしなくても‥‥。
English: I-it's okay. I understand.

Japanese: うん。‥‥それに、 あたし、聞いちゃったから。
English: And... I've also heard about you.

Japanese: え？　何を？
English: Heard...? Heard what about me?

Japanese: 実は、この前、お姉ちゃんと 電話で話したの‥‥。
English: I... was talking to my sister on the phone the other day...

Japanese: “今日はね、私の部下の 初舞台だったの”
English: "Today was my junior partner's first time in court."

Japanese: へー！　そうなんだ。 で、で、どうだった？
English: Wow! Really? How'd that go?

Japanese: “それはもう、あらゆる意味で 大騒ぎだったわ”
English: "It was quite the scene!"

Japanese: “正直、ヤバかったわ。 ‥‥こんなこと、ひさしぶり”
English: "Honestly, I was on edge the whole time. It's been a while..."

Japanese: えー！　そうなんだ。 デキの悪いブカなんだね？
English: Hah! So, he crashed and burned?

Japanese: “‥‥彼は天才よ”
English: "...He's a genius."

Japanese: “まさに《恐怖のツッコミ男》と いったところかしら”
English: "One of those 'strike fear into the hearts of evil' types..."

Japanese: “彼にたりないのは、 ‥‥そう、ケイケンだけ”
English: "The only thing he's lacking is... experience."

Japanese: ふーん。
English: Huh, sounds like it was fun!

Japanese: じゃあ、あたしに何かあったら、 おねがいしちゃおうかな！
English: Well, I know who to go to if I ever get into trouble now!

Japanese: “いい？　真宵”
English: "I don't know, Maya."

Japanese: “今は、まだダメ。 あと３年待ちなさい”
English: "I think you might want to wait... give him three more years."

Japanese: “無罪になりたかったら‥‥ね”
English: "That is, unless you want to be found guilty."

Japanese: ‥‥って。
English: That's what she said!

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　す、すみません。
English: I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: シツレイなこと言っちゃって。
English: I didn't mean to trouble you...

Japanese: い、いやいや。 ホントのことだし。
English: No, it's okay. It's true, I guess.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、やっぱり じっとしていられないんだ。
English: But... at the same time, I can't just sit and watch!

Japanese: 千尋さんを、あんな目にあわせた ヤツのこと、考えると。
English: When I think of the person who did this to Mia...

Japanese: 弁護士さん‥‥。
English: I know...

Japanese: あの‥‥！
ん？　どうしたの？
English: Huh? Something the matter?

Japanese: すみません‥‥。おねがいしたい ことがあるんですけど‥‥。
English: Um... I was wondering, could I ask you a favor?

Japanese: このメモ、えらい弁護士さんの 住所なんです。
English: This is the address of a famous lawyer.

Japanese: 昔、お姉ちゃんが 書いてくれて‥‥。
English: My sister gave me this a long time ago.

Japanese: 困ったときは、この方に頼めば だいじょうぶだ、って。
English: She said if I was ever in trouble, I should call him.

Japanese: それで‥‥、
English: And, well, I'm in trouble.

Japanese: よかったら、弁護をおねがいして いただきたいな、って‥‥。
English: Do you think you could go ask him to represent me?

Japanese: （どうしよう‥‥？）
ああ、いいよ。 頼んできてあげる。
English: Sure, why not? I'll go ask.

Japanese: ありがとうございます！
English: Thank you so much!

Japanese: あたし、他に頼める人が いなくて‥‥。
English: I have no one else to turn to...

Japanese: そういえば、ご両親とかは‥‥？
English: Say, what about your parents?

Japanese: そ、そうか。 ‥‥うん。ぼくにまかせて。
English: I... I see. Don't worry, leave it to me.

Japanese: お願いします。 ‥‥明日、１０時からです。 at 10:00.
English: Thank you! The trial's tomorrow...

Japanese: え、ええっ！　‥‥あ、明日！
English: W-what!? Tomorrow!?

Japanese: はい。
English: Tomorrow.

Japanese: も、もしこの人に断られたら、 どうするんだよ！
English: What if this guy refuses!?

Japanese: ‥‥悪いけど、
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: そういうことは、 自分でやった方がいいと思うよ。
English: I think this is the kind of thing you should really do yourself.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですよね。
English: ...You're right.

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: Something wrong?

Japanese: 実は、警察の人におねがいして、 何度も連絡してみたんです。
English: Actually, I asked the police to contact him, and they tried calling a few times.

Japanese: 全然‥‥つかまらなくて。
English: Nobody could get a hold of him!

Japanese: つかまらない？
English: They couldn't find him?

Japanese: あたし、もう‥‥ 相談できる人、いないんです。
English: I have no one left to ask!

Japanese: そういえば、ご両親とかは‥‥？
English: Say, what about your parents...?

Japanese: わ、わかった！ ぼくが行ってみるよ。
English: O-okay! Don't worry, I'll go ask him for you.

Japanese: ほ、ほんとですか！ ありがとうございます！
English: Y-you will? Thank you so much!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、もし、その先生に 会えなかったら、どうするの？
English: I'm just worried what will happen if I can't find him.

Japanese: そのときは、国で選んでくれた 弁護士さんが来るそうです。
English: They told me that if I don't find one, the state will pick an attorney to defend me.

Japanese: それは、いつ？
English: When will that happen?

Japanese: 今日の４時まで 待ってもらっています。
English: They're giving me until 4:00 this afternoon.

Japanese: （面会時間、ギリギリだな‥‥。 もう、時間がないぞ）
English: (And visiting hours are almost up... I'd better hurry!)

Japanese: わかった。また来るよ！
English: Right, I'll be back!

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。 さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch. A real pro, this guy.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: 事件があった日のこと、 聞かせてくれないかな。
English: Could you tell me about the day of the murder?

Japanese: はい！
English: Yes!

Japanese: ‥‥ええと。あの日の朝、 お姉ちゃんから電話がありました。
English: Let's see... that morning, I got a call from my sister.

Japanese: 今度の裁判の証拠品を あずかってくれ、‥‥って。
English: She wanted me to hold onto a piece of evidence for an upcoming trial.

Japanese: 証拠品？
English: Evidence?

Japanese: はい。‥‥《考える人》の置時計。
English: Yes. That clock shaped like "The Thinker."

Japanese: （‥‥矢張の作った、 例のヤツか。）
English: (The one Larry made...)

Japanese: でも、なんであんなのが 証拠品なのかなあ‥‥？
English: How could that have been evidence in a case?

Japanese: えーと‥‥ なんででしたっけ‥‥。
English: Um, right, she said something about that...

Japanese: あっ、そうだ！
English: I remember!

Japanese: よかったら、そのときの お姉ちゃんの話、聞きますか？
English: Do you want to hear it in her own voice?

Japanese: えっ、聞けるの？
English: H-her own voice!?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes.

Japanese: あの、あたしの携帯電話に、 そのときの会話が‥‥
English: I'm pretty sure our conversation is on my cell phone.

Japanese: 録音してあるんだ！
English: You recorded it!

Japanese: はい！ ‥‥消し方を知らないから‥‥。
English: Yeah! I forgot how to delete those things.

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。前から ちょっと気になってたんだけど。
English: There's something I've been wanting to ask you...

Japanese: はい？
English: Yes?

Japanese: どうして そんなカッコウを‥‥？
English: What's with that outfit?

Japanese: あ、これですか？
English: Oh, this?

Japanese: これは、見習いの修行者が 着る‥‥装束の一種、です。
English: This is what all acolytes wear. It's my uniform, you could say.

Japanese: 見習い？　修行者？ ‥‥きみはいったい、なんなんだ？
English: A-acolytes? Like people in religious training? What is it you do?

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　べつにあたし、 アヤシイ者じゃないですよ！
English: Oh! It's nothing strange, really!

Japanese: ただの霊媒師です。 ‥‥修行中の。
English: I'm a spirit medium. ...In training.

Japanese: れ、れいばいしぃ？ （じゅうぶんアヤシイ‥‥）
English: A s-spirit medium!? (I'm pretty sure that qualifies as strange.)

Japanese: きみは、修行中の身なんだ‥‥ その、霊媒師の。
English: So you're an acolyte. A, er, medium-in-training.

Japanese: そうですよ。
English: That's right.

Japanese: 綾里家は、とっても 霊力が強い家系で、特に女性は‥‥
English: The Fey family, especially the women, have always been very sensitive to the spirit world.

Japanese: ちょっと待って‥‥！ あ、“綾里家”？
English: Wait a second, you said the "Fey Family"?

Japanese: ってことは‥‥千尋さんも？
English: So, Mia was into this stuff too?

Japanese: もちろん！
English: Of course!

Japanese: “きゃりあうーまんになる”って、 急に山を下りちゃったけど‥‥
English: She left the mountain to "follow her career," she said.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの霊力、ホント、 シャレにならなかったんですよ。
English: Her powers were first- class, too!

Japanese: （し‥‥知らなかった‥‥ あの千尋さんが‥‥）
English: (I... I had no idea.)

Japanese: ん？
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥待てよ。
English: Wait...!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What?

Japanese: ホンモノの霊媒師なんだよね？
English: So, you're a real, honest- to-goodness spirit medium? With E.S.P. and all that?

Japanese: はい。 ‥‥修行中ですけど。
English: Yes. ...In training.

Japanese: じゃあ、千尋さんの霊を 呼び出せばいいじゃないか！
English: Well, can't you contact Mia's spirit, then?

Japanese: 本人に直接、 誰に殺されたか、聞いてみようよ。
English: We can just ask her who killed her!

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥。
English: I-I'm sorry...

Japanese: なにぶん、 修行中の身ですから‥‥。
English: I'm still in training.

Japanese: そんな大それたこと、 とてもとても‥‥。
English: I couldn't do something on that level...

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。 そんなウマい話、ないか）
English: (Hmm... I thought that would be too easy.)

Japanese: その、携帯電話に録音されている 会話だけど、
English: So, you say you have a conversation with your sister on your cell phone?

Japanese: さっそく、聞かせてよ！
English: Let's hear it!

Japanese: はい！
English: Right!

Japanese: ‥‥あれ？
English: Oh!

Japanese: そうだ。電話、あの刑事さんが 持っていっちゃいました。
English: I just remembered: that detective took my cell phone.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません‥‥。
English: Sorry.

Japanese: あ、そう。 （‥‥そりゃ、そうだな）
English: Oh, right. (Of course...)

Japanese: わかった。今度、刑事さんに 会ったら、聞いてみるよ。
English: Next time I see Detective Gumshoe I'll ask him for it.

Japanese: じゃあ、わすれないように、 メモを書きますね。
English: I'll write you a note so you don't forget, okay?

Japanese: あ、ありがとう。
English: Sure, thanks.

Japanese: 《真宵のメモ》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Maya's Memo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません。 特に心当たり、ないです。
English: Sorry... I've never seen that before.

Japanese: これ、所長のそばに 散らばってたんだけど‥‥
English: This was lying next to the chief's body...

Japanese: はい。 あたしも見ました。
English: I saw that too.

Japanese: たぶん、電気スタンドの カケラかな、って。
English: They said they thought these were pieces of a broken light stand.

Japanese: うん。たぶん、そうだね。
English: Yeah, that seems about right.

Japanese: （でも、ガラス製の電気スタンド なんて、めずらしいな‥‥）
English: (Though I'd never heard of a glass light stand before this...)

Japanese: あの、凶器のことなんだけど。
English: I wanted to ask you about the murder weapon...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、かわいそう‥‥。
English: Poor Sis... *sniff*

Japanese: （‥‥これについては、 あまり聞かない方がいいな）
English: (Hmm. Better not ask her about this now.)

Japanese: 携帯電話のことだけど‥‥。
English: I wanted to ask you about your cell phone...

Japanese: あの刑事さんが 持っていっちゃいました。
English: That detective took it when they brought me in.

Japanese: （あの刑事か‥‥）
English: (So, Gumshoe took it...)

Japanese: （今度見かけたら、返してくれって 頼んでみよう‥‥）
English: (I'll have to try to get it back next time I see him...)

Japanese: あの領収書、覚えてる？
English: Remember that receipt?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あたしの名前が 書いてあったやつですか。
English: Y-you mean the one with my name on it?

Japanese: 心当たりは‥‥
English: Any idea why she...

Japanese: ゼッタイありません！
English: Absolutely none!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Um...

Japanese: 信じてくれますか？
English: Do you trust me?

Japanese: 信じるよ。
English: I trust you.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてですか？
English: Why?

Japanese: あたしがやったんだ‥‥って、 考えないんですか？
English: Don't you think I did it, too?

Japanese: 考えない。
English: No, I don't.

Japanese: （‥‥カン、だけどね）
English: (It's just a hunch, but...)

Japanese: ‥‥刑事さんたちは、 信じてくれなかった‥‥。
English: That detective thinks I did it.

Japanese: やっぱり、 信じてくれないんですね。
English: I didn't think you'd believe me.

Japanese: 刑事さんたちと、いっしょだ‥‥。
English: You're just like the detective.

Japanese: ‥‥ごめん。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: いいかげんななぐさめを 言うのは、キライなんだ。
English: I don't like lying just to make people feel better.

Japanese: でも、きみがやったと 決めつけてるわけじゃないよ。
English: But it doesn't mean that I've decided you did it!

Japanese: （‥‥あの姿を見てるからな）
English: (She seemed so lost at the crime scene.)

Japanese: あっ！
English: Hey!

Japanese: ど、どうでした？ 弁護士の先生‥‥？
English: What is it? Did you meet the lawyer?

Japanese: ご、ごめん。
English: Sorry.

Japanese: まだ、会えないんだ。
English: I haven't seen him yet.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: （そうだな‥‥。行ってみるか、 エラい弁護士さんの事務所に）
English: (Hmm. I'd better go see if I can find this elite lawyer she's talking about.)

Japanese: 事件があった日のこと、 聞かせてくれないかな。
English: Could you tell me about the day of the murder?

Japanese: ‥‥イヤなこと、思い出させて 悪いけど。
English: Sorry... I know it must be hard.

Japanese: 気にしないでください。
English: No, it's okay.

Japanese: 最近は、その話ばっかり くり返していたから、
English: All I've been doing the last few hours is talking about it.

Japanese: 少し、慣れちゃいました‥‥。
English: I've kind of gotten used to it...

Japanese: ‥‥ええと。あの日の朝、 お姉ちゃんから電話がありました。
English: Let's see... that morning, I got a call from my sister.

Japanese: 今度の裁判の証拠品を あずかってくれっていう話で‥‥。
English: She wanted me to hold on to a piece of evidence for an upcoming trial.

Japanese: （‥‥矢張が作った、 例の《考える人》か）
English: (That's "The Thinker" clock that Larry made.)

Japanese: （ある種、連続殺人犯とも いうべきシロモノだな）
English: (It practically qualifies as a serial murderer by now.)

Japanese: それで、事務所に着いたのが‥‥？
English: So then, when did you arrive at the office?

Japanese: ちょうど、９時ぐらいでした。
English: It was right around 9:00.

Japanese: 電気が消えていて、 そして‥‥血のニオイがして‥‥、
English: The lights were off and... I could smell blood.

Japanese: そ、そして、 お、お、おねえちゃんが‥‥。
English: Th-then I found her. My sister...

Japanese: わ、わかった。
English: Thanks, Maya.

Japanese: もういいよ‥‥ありがとう。
English: That's all I need to hear for now.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。家族は‥‥？
English: What about your family?

Japanese: お姉ちゃんだけ、でした。
English: I only had my sister.

Japanese: 父は、小さいころに亡くなって、
English: My father died when I was very young.

Japanese: 母は、どこにいるのか‥‥ わかりません。
English: And I don't know where my mother is.

Japanese: （わからない‥‥ 死んだわけじゃないのか？）
English: (Don't know...? So she could still be alive?)

Japanese: あたしたち綾里家の女は、代々、 霊媒師として生きてきたんです。
English: The women in my family have been mediums for generations.

Japanese: 綾里の血は、強い霊力を 宿しているから‥‥。
English: They say that E.S.P. runs in our blood.

Japanese: 今から、１５年ぐらい前。
English: About fifteen years ago,

Japanese: あたしたちは、ある事件に 巻き込まれました。
English: our family was involved in an... incident.

Japanese: そして、ある１人の男によって、 母は‥‥
English: There was a man, and he... he...

Japanese: ‥‥母は、破滅‥‥したんです。
English: He ruined our mother's life.
("Ruined"...?)

Japanese: そして母は、姿を 消してしまいました。
English: After that, she disappeared.

Japanese: それから数年後、
English: Several years after that,

Japanese: お姉ちゃんは“弁護士になる”と 言って‥‥山を下りたんです。
English: my sister announced she would "become a lawyer" and she left the mountain.

Japanese: じゃあ、今は１人で‥‥？
English: So, you live by yourself?

Japanese: そうですね。 もう、慣れちゃいましたけど。
English: Yes. I've gotten used to it.

Japanese: それに。
English: Oh, also...

Japanese: あたしが一人前にならなきゃ、 綾里の霊力が消えちゃうし。
English: I had to become independent, or I would lose my E.S.P.!

Japanese: （この子‥‥ずっと、１人ぼっち なのか‥‥）
English: (I feel bad for her, all by herself up on that mountain...)

Japanese: その、きみのお母さんを 破滅させた男、って‥‥？
English: So, who was this man who, um, "ruined" your mother?

Japanese: 今から１５年ぐらい前‥‥。 ふしぎな殺人事件がありました。
English: About 15 years ago... there was an unusual murder case.

Japanese: 世間は、その話題で 大変なさわぎだったそうです。
English: It made quite a stir, everyone was talking about it, apparently.

Japanese: 警察は、どんな手を使ってでも 犯人を逮捕したかった‥‥。
English: The police were running out of leads, and they were getting desperate...

Japanese: まさか‥‥、それで‥‥霊媒を？
English: Wait... they didn't use a spirit medium, did they?

Japanese: 警察に説得されて、 母は、被害者の霊媒をしました。
English: The police convinced my mother to try to contact the victim.

Japanese: へえ、すごいな。 ‥‥じゃあ、事件は？
English: Wow... So, what happened?

Japanese: 解決しました。 ‥‥一度は。
English: The case was solved... we thought.

Japanese: “一度は”？
English: You "thought"...?

Japanese: 母の言葉で逮捕された男は、 無実だったんです。
English: The man my mother helped the police capture was innocent.

Japanese: このことは、すべて極秘で すすめられていました。
English: The police's consultation with a medium had all been carried out in secret, of course.

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But...

Japanese: それをスクープした 男がいたんです。
English: A man found out about it and leaked it to the press.

Japanese: その男は、大々的にマスコミを 動かして、母をインチキだ、って。
English: He told all the papers that my mother was a fraud, and the media jumped on it big time.

Japanese: ‥‥母は‥‥日本中の‥‥ 笑いものに‥‥。
English: She... my mother... became the laughing stock of the nation.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどね。
English: I see.
White...

Japanese: え、コナカ？
English: Excuse me? White?

Japanese: その男の名前。 ‥‥お姉ちゃんから‥‥。
English: That was his name. My sister told me.
White? Hmm...

Japanese: あの、凶器のことなんだけど‥‥。
English: Um, about the weapon...

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、かわいそう‥‥。
English: Poor Sis...

Japanese: （‥‥これについては、 あまり聞かない方がいいな）
English: (Hmm. Best not to talk about that subject right now.)

Japanese: 携帯電話のことだけど‥‥。
English: About your cell phone...

Japanese: ああ‥‥。
English: Yes.

Japanese: あれには、お姉ちゃんと 話したことが録音されています。
English: It has a recording of the conversation I had with my sister on it...

Japanese: そうか‥‥考えてみたら、
English: Say...

Japanese: あれがあれば、またお姉ちゃんの 声が聞けるんだ‥‥。
English: If I listened to that, I could hear my sister's voice again.

Japanese: ほら‥‥携帯電話。 もらってきたよ。
English: Hey, I got your cell phone back.

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。
English: Oh! Say...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの声、 聞かせてもらえますか‥‥？
English: Can I listen to my sister's voice?

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんは、目を閉じて 千尋さんの声に聞き入っている）
English: (Maya's eyes closed... She listened to every word with such intensity...)

Japanese: （やがてその目に涙があふれ、 こぼれ落ちていった）
English: (Before long, tears began to roll down her cheeks.)

Japanese: ありがとう‥‥。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: あの領収書、覚えてる？
English: Do you remember the receipt?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、あたしの名前が 書いてあったやつですか。
English: Oh, you mean the one with my name on it?

Japanese: 心当たりは‥‥
English: Any idea why...

Japanese: ゼッタイありません！
English: Absolutely none!

Japanese: ９月　６日　午後３時４２分 留置所　面会室
English: September 6, 3:42 PM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: や、やあ。
English: Hiya.

Japanese: あっ！ どっ、どうでした！
English: Oh! You're back!

Japanese: 会えました？ ‥‥弁護士の先生に！
English: Did you find the lawyer?

Japanese: うん‥‥まあね。 （どうしよう）
English: Um... well... (What do I tell her?)

Japanese: あ、あのさ。 （とても、正直に話せない！）
English: Well, see... (Just be honest!)

Japanese: や、やめた方がいいと思うなあ、 あの先生だけは。
English: I... I really don't think you should use that guy.

Japanese: ぐあい悪そうだったよ。 ガリガリにヤセ細っていて。
English: He... didn't seem healthy. He was all skin and bones!

Japanese: 何があったんですか？
English: What really happened?

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ 断られたんですか‥‥？
English: You don't mean... He refused to help?
Urp.

Japanese: そうなんだ‥‥。
English: I see.

Japanese: 見捨てられちゃったんですね、 あたし‥‥。
English: I've been abandoned, then.

Japanese: あ。そろそろ、国選弁護士の先生が 来る時間‥‥。
English: Just a little longer now before the state-appointed lawyer comes, I guess...

Japanese: （４時。タイムリミットか）
English: (4:00 PM. Time's up.)

Japanese: （‥‥どうする？）
English: (What should I do?)

Japanese: （この子を見捨てて、 このまま帰るのか？）
English: (Do I just leave her and go home...?)

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくに、できることは もう、ないよな）
English: (There's nothing left here for me to do...)

Japanese: （国選弁護士がついた方が、 この子のためになるはずだ）
English: (She'll be better off with a state-appointed lawyer.)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ。帰るよ。
English: I think I'd better get home now.

Japanese: ‥‥さよなら。
English: Good-bye.

Japanese: そして、数日後。
English: It was a few days later when I found out how the story ended.

Japanese: ぼくは、裁判の結果を 新聞で知った。
English: The result of the trial was in the newspaper.

Japanese: 有罪だった。
English: "Guilty."

Japanese: もう、彼女に会うことは ないだろう‥‥二度と。
English: I'll probably never meet her again.

Japanese: ‥‥これで、 正しかったんだろうか？
English: Did I make the right choice?

Japanese: ほんとうに、 よかったんだろうか？
English: Will I ever know?

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥教えてよ‥‥。
English: Mia... if you can hear me, please, tell me!

Japanese: なんてことになったら どうするんだよ！
English: Not! I can't let that happen!

Japanese: やっぱり、このまま 帰るわけにはいかない！
English: I'm not leaving here until she takes me as her lawyer!

Japanese: ‥‥決めた！
English: I've made up my mind!

Japanese: きみがなんと言おうと、 ぼくが弁護をする。
English: I'm going to defend you whether you want me to or not!

Japanese: どうして？
English: Why?

Japanese: どうしてって？ ‥‥それは、
English: Why? Well...

Japanese: （味方が１人もいない悲しさ、 そして孤独な感じ）
English: (No one is as sad as a person without any friends.)

Japanese: （実はぼくにも、おぼえがあった。 ‥‥遠い、昔の話だ）
English: (I know... I've been there. A long time ago.)

Japanese: （そもそも、ぼくはなぜ、 弁護士になったのか‥‥？）
English: (Why did I become a lawyer in the first place...?)

Japanese: （そういう孤独な人の味方になれる 唯一の存在が、弁護士だからだ）
English: (Because someone has to look out for the people who have no one on their side.)

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya...

Japanese: ぼくは、きみの味方だから。 見捨てることは、できない。
English: I won't abandon you. You can count on me.

Japanese: 犯人はきみじゃない。 他にいる。
English: You aren't the culprit! Someone else is!

Japanese: え‥‥。 どうして、わかるんですか？
English: H-how do you know?

Japanese: う。‥‥そんな気が、する。
English: I, um, I have a hunch...

Japanese: （たしかに、この真宵ちゃんを 犯人と考えるのは、カンタンだ）
English: (Given the evidence, it would be easy to assume that Maya was the killer.)

Japanese: （でも、なんとなく気になる ことがあるのも、たしかだ）
English: (But there's something about this whole thing that smells... fishy.)

Japanese: （ぼくをバカにしたような、 あの妙な目撃証人‥‥）
English: (That witness's strange behavior... was that all an act?)

Japanese: （依頼を断った弁護士の、 あの不自然な態度‥‥）
English: (And the way that lawyer refused to help out Maya...)

Japanese: （そして何より、この子には 味方が１人もいない）
English: (But more than all that, she has no one left to help her!)

Japanese: （味方が１人もいない悲しさ、 そして孤独な感じ）
English: (Nothing is more sad, or more lonely than that.)

Japanese: （実はぼくにも、おぼえがあった。 ‥‥遠い、昔の話だ）
English: (I know... I've been there. A long, long time ago.)

Japanese: （そもそも、ぼくはなぜ、 弁護士になったのか‥‥？）
English: (Why did I become a lawyer in the first place...?)

Japanese: （そういう孤独な人の味方になれる 唯一の存在が、弁護士だからだ）
English: (Because someone has to look out for the people who have no one on their side.)

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya...

Japanese: ぼくは、きみの味方だ。 ぜったい見捨てたりしない。
English: I won't abandon you. You can count on me.

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ、 よくわからないよ。
English: To be honest, I don't know.

Japanese: わからない‥‥？
English: You don't know?

Japanese: （目の前の少女は、 本当に犯人なのか‥‥？）
English: (Is this girl sitting in front of me the killer?)

Japanese: （証拠品は、そうであることを 示しているように見える）
English: (All the evidence seems to say "yes.")

Japanese: （だが、なんとなく気になる ことがあるのも、たしかだ）
English: (But there's something about this whole thing that smells fishy.)

Japanese: （ぼくをバカにしたような、 あの妙な目撃証人‥‥）
English: (That witness's strange behavior... was that all an act?)

Japanese: （依頼を断った弁護士の、 あの不自然な態度‥‥）
English: (And the way that lawyer refused to help out Maya...)

Japanese: （そして何より、この子には 味方が１人もいない）
English: (But more than all that, she has no one left to help her!)

Japanese: （味方が１人もいない悲しさ、 そして孤独な感じ）
English: (Nothing is more sad, or more lonely than that.)

Japanese: （実はぼくにも、おぼえがあった。 ‥‥遠い、昔の話だ）
English: (I know... I've been there. A long, long time ago.)

Japanese: （そもそも、ぼくはなぜ、 弁護士になったのか‥‥？）
English: (Why did I become a lawyer in the first place...?)

Japanese: （そういう孤独な人の味方になれる 唯一の存在が、弁護士だからだ）
English: (Because someone has to look out for the people who have no one on their side.)

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくの中で、１つだけ ハッキリしてることがあるよ。
English: There's only one thing I know for certain.

Japanese: ぼくは、きみの味方だ。 見捨てることは、できない。
English: I won't abandon you. You can count on me.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護士さん‥‥。
English: That's so kind of you...
*sniff*...

Japanese: ‥‥さて。
English: Well!

Japanese: そういうことだから。 よろしくね。
English: Let's fight this one and get you out of here!

Japanese: は、はい！ こちらこそ、おねがいします！
English: R-right! Thank you!

Japanese: （なんか、見違えるように 元気になったぞ）
English: (Whew, she smiled at last. She looks like an entirely different person!)

Japanese: 最後に確認するけど、 “無実”と信じていいんだね？
English: One last question... You are innocent, right?

Japanese: はい！
English: Yes!

Japanese: あたし、弁護士さんを信じます。
English: And I trust you...

Japanese: だから、弁護士さんも‥‥。
English: So you trust me, too, okay?

Japanese: うん。わかった。
English: It's a deal.
(So, what next...)

Japanese: （こうなると、確認しなきゃ ならないことがあるな）
English: (There's something that's been bugging me...)

Japanese: （あのアヤシイ女の、 引き出しの中‥‥）
English: (Just what was inside that strange woman's drawer?)

Japanese: （あの女の態度が変わったのは、 引き出しを調べたときだけだ）
English: (It was when I tried to look into the drawer that she got all defensive.)

Japanese: （何かあるに、ちがいない！）
English: (There has to be something in there!)

Japanese: ほら‥‥携帯電話。 もらってきたよ。
English: Hey, I got your cell phone back.

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥。
English: Oh! Say...

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの声、 聞かせてもらえますか‥‥？
English: Can I listen to my sister's voice?

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんは、目を閉じて 千尋さんの声に聞き入っている）
English: (Maya's eyes closed... She listened to every word with such intensity...)

Japanese: （やがてその目に涙があふれ、 こぼれ落ちていった）
English: (Before long, tears began to roll down her cheeks.)

Japanese: ありがとう‥‥。
English: Thank you.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、真宵ちゃんは 今、取り調べを受けてるみたいだ。
English: Hmm... Maya's not here. She must be in questioning.

Japanese: しばらく、会えそうにないな。
English: They probably won't let her out for a while.

Japanese: とりあえず、あの弁護士先生の話、 もう少し聞いておくか‥‥。
English: Maybe I'd better go talk to that lawyer again.

Japanese: ９月　６日　某時刻 板東ホテル　３０３号室
English: September 6 Gatewater Hotel - Room 303

Japanese: あらあ。 いらっしゃーい。
English: Well! Hello there, handsome.

Japanese: は、はあ。 （なんか、調子くるうなあ）
English: Umm... hi. (Smooth, Wright, real smooth.)

Japanese: あなた、弁護士さんよね？ 刑事さんから、お電話あったわ。
English: You're the lawyer, aren't you? The detective told me...

Japanese: “なんにもしゃべっちゃダメッス” ‥‥って。
English: He said, "Don't say nothing to that lawyer, pal!" Tee hee!

Japanese: （あの刑事‥‥よけいなこと、 してくれるよ‥‥）
English: (Memo to self: thank Detective Gumshoe for making my job harder.)

Japanese: なんかぁ、映画みたいねぇ！
English: Gee! This is all like something out of a movie!

Japanese: 梅世、ちょっぴりコーフンぎみ？ みたいな。
English: It's all so exciting I can hardly contain myself!

Japanese: あん。ちょっと待っててね。 お化粧してくるから。
English: Ooh! Let me go freshen up so I can look the part of the beautiful eyewitness!

Japanese: （“あん”って言われても‥‥）
English: (I pity the lawyer that has to cross-examine this one.)

Japanese: （この引き出し、ドライバーが 挟まっているけど‥‥）
English: (There's a screwdriver stuck in this drawer.)

Japanese: （何が入ってるんだ？ 見てみよう‥‥）
English: (I wonder what's inside? Let's take a look...)

Japanese: ちょ、ちょちょっと！
English: Hey! H-h-hey!!!

Japanese: 何してんのよアンタ！ さわるんじゃないわよ！
English: What are you doing!? No touching!

Japanese: あ、ああら‥‥いやぁん。
English: Oooh. Bad boy!

Japanese: ひ、ヒトの部屋を カッテにいじるなんて、
English: Y-you really shouldn't pry around in other people's rooms, now.

Japanese: 梅世、信じらんなぁい！
English: You wouldn't want to make me upset, would you?

Japanese: （い、今、一瞬だけ、 カオつきが変わったぞ！）
English: (Upset!? I thought she was going to explode for a second there!)

Japanese: （あの引き出し‥‥ 何が入ってるんだろう‥‥？）
English: (I wonder what could be inside the drawer?)

Japanese: 思ったとおり、造花だ。
English: The flowers are fake, as expected.

Japanese: ひまわりとチューリップ以外、 ぼくは花の名前は知らない。
English: I know sunflowers and tulips, but that's about the extent of my floral knowledge.

Japanese: テーブルの上には、ボトルと ２つのグラス。
English: A bottle and two glasses are on the table.

Japanese: 誰か、いっしょに 泊まっているのだろうか。
English: Somebody must be staying with her.

Japanese: 窓からは、夏の終わりの日差しが 降りそそいでいる。
English: The late summer sunlight streams through the window.

Japanese: すぐ目の前には、もちろん 綾里法律事務所が見える。
English: There's the Fey & Co. Law Offices building, of course.

Japanese: たしかに、部屋の中まで 丸見えだけど‥‥、
English: You can see the inside of the room pretty clearly from here.

Japanese: 誰がいるか、まで見わけるのは むずかしいと思う。
English: I think it would be a little difficult to recognize a face from this distance, though.

Japanese: シンプルなデザインの ベッドだ。
English: A simple bed.

Japanese: きちんと整えられていて、 目を引くところはない。
English: It's been recently made. Nothing eye-catching here.

Japanese: 静物画‥‥あれ？　“生物画” だったっけ？
English: Ah. A still-scene painting. Wait, should that be "still life"?

Japanese: まあ、そんなような絵が かかっている。
English: Whatever. One of those is hanging on the wall.

Japanese: （何が入ってるのか、 気になるなあ‥‥）
English: (Hmm... what's inside I wonder?)

Japanese: コホン、コホン。
English: *cough* *cough*

Japanese: （‥‥今はダメだな‥‥）
English: (Maybe later...)

Japanese: 事件があったとき あなたが何を見たのか、
English: Do you think you could tell me something?

Japanese: 聞かせてくれませんか？
English: I need you to describe what you observed at the time of the incident.

Japanese: えー。いやぁん、 そんなカタ苦しい聞きかた。
English: Ooh. "Observe," "incident"! You sound just like a lawyer in the movies!

Japanese: もっと、なかよく やりましょうよぅ‥‥。
English: I like a man with a big... vocabulary.

Japanese: （‥‥うむむ）
English: (Umm... *gulp* Better not encourage her.)

Japanese: あー。事件があったときの ことなんだけどさあ。
English: Er... you know that thing that occur... um... happened the other day? The bad thing?

Japanese: きみー、何か 見ちゃったんだろお？
English: What did you see when it happened?

Japanese: ボクにさあ、 教えてくれないかあ？
English: I don't suppose you could tell me about it? Pretty please?
Let me see... Um, well... Dream on!

Japanese: 聞きたかったら、明日の 法廷に来ればぁ。
English: If you want to know, you'll just have to come to the court tomorrow, Mr. Lawyer!
(Oh boy.)

Japanese: あの。そもそもあなたは、 どういう方なんですか？
English: Um, could you... just who exactly are you?

Japanese: うわあ！　なあに、 クドいてるの、あたしをー？
English: Oooh, Mr. Lawyer! Are you hitting on me?

Japanese: ち、ち、ちがいますよ！ し、仕事で聞いてるんです！
English: N-n-no! Hey! I'm just doing my job here!

Japanese: きゃー、かっわいー。 テレちゃってもお！
English: Tee hee! You know, you're cute when you blush.

Japanese: （‥‥今だかつて、ここまで 赤面したことがあっただろうか）
English: (Believe me, this is the first time in my life I've blushed this much...)

Japanese: え、えへん。
English: Umm... eh heh.

Japanese: えー。し、仕事は、何を されてるんですか。
English: Right... can you just tell me what it is you do?

Japanese: ‥‥教えない。
English: Well...

Japanese: バッカみたい。 言うワケ、ないでしょー。
English: No! Tee hee! And you had your little hopes up, didn't you!
(Oh boy.)

Japanese: あの、テーブルに グラスが２つあるけど、
English: I see there are two glasses on the table.

Japanese: 誰か、いっしょに 泊まってるんですか？
English: Is someone staying here with you?

Japanese: うっわあ、スルドい推理！
English: Oooh! What amazing powers of observation!

Japanese: あなたって、メータンテー？ みたいな！
English: You must be one of those famous detectives, like on television!

Japanese: い、いやあ、そんな。 大したことじゃあ‥‥。
English: Oh, no, not me, I'm, er, just a lawyer!

Japanese: 探偵は、ウラでゴミでも あさってればぁ。
English: Say, Mr. Big Detective, why don't you go look for clues... in the garbage? Hmm?

Japanese: 梅世、センサクされるの、 好きくない？　みたいな。
English: Miss May doesn't like nosey little lawyers... Hmph!
(Oh boy.)

Japanese: あたし、証人なのよ？ ケンサツ側の。わかるぅ？
English: Excuse me, but I'm a witness? Police witness? You understand?

Japanese: あなたの役に立つ情報なんか、 あげるわけないでしょー？
English: How could I possibly give you any information in good conscience, hmm?

Japanese: 刑事さんに“めっ”て 言われちゃうしぃ。
English: Me... "the witness"! It's just like the movie!

Japanese: いらっしゃいませ。
English: Good afternoon, sir!

Japanese: あ、あなたは？
English: Excuse me, you are...?

Japanese: あ。申し遅れました。
English: Ah, I beg your pardon, sir!

Japanese: ワタクシ、このホテルの ボーイでございます。
English: I am the bellboy of this establishment, at your service, sir.

Japanese: は、はあ。
English: Oh, right.

Japanese: ルームサービスをお届けに 上がったしだいでございます。
English: I've just come up to deliver room service, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。梅世さんは？
English: Um... do you know where Miss May might be?

Japanese: ‥‥は。お客様は今、 バスルームでございます。
English: Ah. I believe our guest Miss May is currently using the, er, facilities...?

Japanese: では。シツレイいたします。
English: If you've no need of anything, I'll be taking my leave.

Japanese: ま。どうぞごゆっくり、 おたのしみを‥‥。
English: Please, stay as long as you like. Enjoy...

Japanese: え。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: い、いや違う‥‥！
English: Wait... no! Hey!

Japanese: （‥‥ここに来るたび、 ヒドい目にあってばかりだ）
English: (Why does it seem like every time I come here, I end up embarrassing myself?)

Japanese: （しかし、部屋を調べる 絶好のチャンスだな！）
English: (Wait... now's my chance to snoop around a bit!)

Japanese: あ、そうそう、そうでした。
English: Ah, I almost forgot!

Japanese: わっ！ ‥‥な、何？
English: Gah! Y-you came back quick!

Japanese: お客さまに、お電話があったことを 伝えていただけますか？
English: Might I ask you to inform Miss May that there is a message for her?

Japanese: 株式会社《コナカルチャー》の 小中様からでございます。
English: Please tell her that Mr. White, of Bluecorp phoned.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Oh, right. Sure.

Japanese: （“コナカルチャーの小中”‥‥？ なんか、引っかかる名前だな）
English: (Mr. White... of Bluecorp? Where have I heard that name?)
White...

Japanese: その男の名前。 ‥‥お姉ちゃんから‥‥。
English: That was his name. My sister told me.

Japanese: （‥‥お母さんを破滅させた 男の名前‥‥か‥‥）
English: ("White" was the name of the guy who ruined Mia and Maya's mother!)

Japanese: （‥‥コ・ナ・カ‥‥。 偶然なのか‥‥？）
English: (Could it be a coincidence?)

Japanese: やっぱり、 松竹　梅世はアヤシイぞ！
English: There is definitely something suspicious about this "Miss May"!

Japanese: こんなものを持って、 ホテルに泊まっているなんて‥‥。
English: Why would she have something like this in her hotel room?

Japanese: ぜったい、何かウラがあるに 違いない！
English: There's a story behind all this, I know it!

Japanese: よし‥‥。
English: Alright...

Japanese: 明日の法廷は、コイツも武器にして 戦ってやるぞ！
English: I'll be using this bit of evidence in tomorrow's trial, that's for sure.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんのためにも‥‥
English: For Maya's sake...

Japanese: 絶対に、この女の正体を あばいてやる！
English: I'll get to this woman's bottom! Wait... I mean ... you know what I mean.

Japanese: ボーイさあん。 ‥‥まだいるのぉ？
English: Oh, bellboy...? Still there?

Japanese: （おっと。そろそろ退散するか！）
English: (Uh oh, time to scram!)

Japanese: （明日、また遊ぼうぜ梅世さん！）
English: (I look forward to tangoing with you tomorrow, Miss May!)

Japanese: （‥‥法廷でな！）
English: (In court!)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ドライバーが引っかかって 引き出しが半開きになっている。
English: There's a screwdriver sticking out of that half-open drawer.

Japanese: 今のうち、中を調べておこう‥‥
English: Now's my chance to see what's inside!

Japanese: こ、これは！
English: What do we have here!

Japanese: と、盗聴器‥‥だよな。 間違いなく。
English: A... wiretap? Hmm!

Japanese: あの女、なんでこんなものを‥‥？
English: What would a woman like her be doing with a thing like this?

Japanese: 証拠品《盗聴器》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Wiretap added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ９月　６日　某時刻 星影法律事務所
English: September 6 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: （受付によると、大先生は “外出中”らしい）
English: (According to the receptionist, the big boss is "out".)

Japanese: （いつ戻ってくるかは わからないそうだ）
English: (She couldn't say when he'd be back.)

Japanese: （‥‥それにしても、 ヤリ手の弁護士ともなると、）
English: (It must be hard to keep track of everything when you're a famous lawyer...)

Japanese: （事務所も大変なことに なってるなあ）
English: (Not to mention run an office like this.)

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、またあとで 来てみるか‥‥）
English: (I guess I'll just have to come back later.)

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ。大先生は お出かけ中、か‥‥）
English: (Hmm... seems like Mr. Grossberg is out.)

Japanese: （しかたない、もう少ししてから また来るか‥‥）
English: (Well, maybe I should just wait here for him to come back.)

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: *Ah-HHHHEM!*

Japanese: （お‥‥この、エラそうなヒト 特有のセキ払いは‥‥）
English: (If that wasn't the most over-the-top clearing of the throat I've ever heard!)

Japanese: あー、チミかね。 ワシを待っておるというのは。
English: Ah hah! So, you're the one they say has been looking for me?

Japanese: あ、は、はい。 （思った以上にエラそうな先生だ）
English: Uh... y-yes, that's me! (He looks even... grander than I imagined!)

Japanese: ん‥‥そのエリのバッジ‥‥。
English: Hmm...? That badge on your collar...?

Japanese: なんぢゃい。弁護士かねチミも。
English: Ah, so you're a lawyer, are you now?

Japanese: え、ええ、まあ。
English: Y-yes, well... yes.

Japanese: ‥‥で、何かねチミ？
English: And what do you want?

Japanese: 最近はヒマだから、話を 聞いてやろうぢゃないか。
English: I'm not particularly busy these days... Please, proceed!

Japanese: （‥‥ヒマだって？　じゃあ、 なぜ連絡がとれなかったんだ？）
English: (Not busy...? Then how come no one could get in touch with you?)

Japanese: ん？　どうした？
English: Hmm? Something the matter?

Japanese: この星影　宇宙ノ介 （ほしかげそらのすけ）が、
English: You came to see the one-and-only Marvin Grossberg, did you not?

Japanese: 話を聞いてやろうと 言っとるんぢゃよチミ？
English: Well, here I am, boy! What do you want? Out with it!

Japanese: あの‥‥実は、綾里　真宵さんの件 で来たんですけど‥‥。
English: Um... w-well, sir, actually, it's about Maya. Maya Fey.

Japanese: あ、綾里　真宵‥‥ねえ。
English: Ah... yes. Maya Fey. Go on.

Japanese: （‥‥なんだ？ ようすがオカシイぞ‥‥）
English: (Hmm? Why the strange reaction?)

Japanese: あー‥‥。
English: A-cha-cha.

Japanese: ワシは、そう。 忙しいんぢゃよチミ。
English: I'm really quite busy here, son.

Japanese: いきなり明日と言われても‥‥。 ムリぢゃな、ムリ。
English: I can't go taking cases on a day's notice! No, it's quite impossible.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: W-wait a second!

Japanese: なぜ、“明日”って 知ってるんですか？
English: How did you know the trial was tomorrow!?

Japanese: むぐぐ‥‥。
English: Urk? Ahem!

Japanese: と、とにかく。
English: A-anyway...

Japanese: ダメぢゃ。その件だけは、 ぜーったい、引き受けられん！
English: I'm afraid it's entirely impossible for me to represent her. Sorry. End of discussion.

Japanese: （ど、どうなってるんだ？ 話が違うぞ‥‥！）
English: (What's going on!? He refused me before I even got a chance to ask him!)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんに、なんて言えば いいんだよ‥‥）
English: (What do I tell Maya...?)

Japanese: さっきから、ずっと 気になってたんだよ、あの絵。
English: That painting has been bugging me ever since I stepped in here.

Japanese: あつくるしいほどコッテリした アブラ絵だ。
English: The oil paint is so thick it's practically giving me a stuffed nose.

Japanese: お値段の方も、さぞかし コッテリしているんだろうな。
English: I'm sure the price is nothing to sneeze at either, for that matter.

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... they don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: どうして断るんですか！ 理由を聞かせてください！
English: How can you just refuse like that! Please, tell me why you won't take the case!

Japanese: い、いや、その‥‥ だから、忙しい‥‥からぢゃ。
English: Hmm? Eh, ahem! Well, you see it's just... I'm busy, you see!

Japanese: 依頼人は、綾里　千尋さんの 妹さんですよ！
English: But the client is Mia Fey's sister!

Japanese: う‥‥うむむ‥‥。
English: Hmm... ahem.

Japanese: 千尋さんは、あなたを信頼して 彼女をお願いしたんです。
English: Mia trusted you... She knew her sister would be in good hands.

Japanese: そんなことは、わかっておる‥‥。
English: Yes, yes, of course I know that.

Japanese: ぢゃが‥‥
English: However!

Japanese: すまないが、 引き受けるわけには、いかん。
English: I'm sorry but, I must refuse. Sorry. Good-bye.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドい人だな‥‥。
English: Creep.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Fine.

Japanese: 時間がない。 ‥‥他を当たることにします。
English: I don't have time to argue with you anyway. I'll go look elsewhere.

Japanese: ‥‥ぢゃ。
English: *grumble*... Think not.

Japanese: ？　何か言いましたか？
English: Huh? Did you say something?

Japanese: ‥‥ムダぢゃ、と言った。
English: I think not, I said.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: Wh-what do you mean?

Japanese: 気の毒ぢゃが‥‥その依頼、
English: I'm terribly, terribly sorry.

Japanese: まともな弁護士ならば、決して 引き受けることはない。
English: But I'm afraid that no lawyer worth their salt will take on this particular case.

Japanese: ‥‥まこと、モウシワケないが。
English: Terribly sorry, m'boy.

Japanese: な、なぜです！
English: Why!?

Japanese: それは‥‥言えん。 I...
English: I cannot say.

Japanese: 悪いが、帰ってくれんか。 もう話すことはない。
English: I beg your pardon, but could you leave? Now? I've nothing more to discuss with you.

Japanese: （‥‥どういうことなんだ！）
English: (What's going on here!?)

Japanese: 千尋さんとは、どういう‥‥？
English: How did you know Mia Fey...?

Japanese: 彼女は、ここにおったんぢゃよ。 ‥‥昔な。
English: She... worked here. A long time ago.

Japanese: すごい子ぢゃった‥‥。
English: Quite the apprentice, that one.

Japanese: あっという間に、ワシの テクニックを身につけてな。
English: Learned my techniques in the blink of an eye!

Japanese: ある日、突然 ここを出て行きおった‥‥。
English: She left one day, quite suddenly...

Japanese: あの子には“やるべきこと”が あったんぢゃ。
English: She had a mission, you see.

Japanese: “やるべきこと”‥‥？
English: A "mission"?

Japanese: あの子の目はいつも、それを まっすぐに見すえておったよ。
English: You could see it in her eyes.

Japanese: 決して、 ゆらぐことはなかった。
English: She followed it with a burning passion. Never looked back, that one.

Japanese: あの‥‥すごい絵ですね。
English: That's... quite a painting.

Japanese: おお！　よく聞いてくれたな！
English: Ahah! You noticed!

Japanese: ワシのごじまんの絵ぢゃ！ すんばらしいぢゃろ？　ぢゃろ？
English: It's my pride and joy! Impressive, isn't it? Well? ISN'T IT?

Japanese: この空の色！　この海の色！ このムギワラぼうしの色！
English: The color of the sky! The hue of the sea! The weave of the straw hat!

Japanese: ３億か４億の値がついとるが、 ワシゃ、手ばなすつもりはないぞ！
English: It's worth at least three million. I have no intention of parting with it, of course.

Japanese: ああ。ぜーったい、売らん！ ‥‥チミにもな！
English: No, I won't sell it! Not even to you!

Japanese: （買うつもりもないよ‥‥）
English: (I wasn't interested...)

Japanese: 売らんぞ！
English: It's not for sale!

Japanese: 買いません！
English: I'm not buying! (Geez!)

Japanese: 悪いが、この件については、 何も言うことはできんのぢゃ。
English: Very sorry, but I've got nothing to say regarding this matter.
